_comment_llvm_triple: x86_64-unknown-uefi is rejected by compiler-rt's int_lib.h (neither __ELF__ nor __MINGW32__ nor _WIN32 is defined for it). x86_64-pc-windows-gnu makes Clang define __MINGW32__, compiler-rt is happy, and the output format is still PE/COFF, which is what we want for UEFI. We set the EFI subsystem in the linker step.
_comment_linker: TinyGo only knows the ELF flavor of lld (ld.lld). We use it to produce the relocatable .o; the final PE32+ EFI binary is built by an external lld-link invocation (see Taskfile).
